title: How to delete all updates for all items in a table in one go
excerpt:
Hello, 
is there a way to bulk-delete all updates posted in the “conversation” of all items on a board? 
thanks!

posts.author: BenH (Benjamin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello,

is there a way to bulk-delete all updates posted in the “conversation” of all items on a board?

thanks!

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @BenH,
Currently, I don’t think there is a way to do that. You will have to delete them individually.
The alternative is, you can use the Duplicates and Uniques app to copy your board items to another board. At the moment, the copy and move functionality leaves out the updates on items.
After copying, you can then delete your original board with the updates.

Here is a video that explains how the copy functionality works.

If you have any question, you can react out at support@kolaai.com

posts.author: BenH (Benjamin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Kolaai,

thanks for the response.

With that inspiration I tried dupliucating the board via the built-in option directly in monday and that actually gives me the choice to duplicate

  • the structure
  • the structure and the items
  • the structure and the items and the updates

So if I use the second option I achieve pretty much what I was after.

Thank you

posts.author: kolaai (alfred)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes! Actually forgot about that option. I’m happy you were able to draw inspiration from the answer and come with yours. Well done.

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
